def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Predict the next frame of a 64x64 grid game.

    Observations show that ACTION5 changes cells in row 53:
        any cell with value 2 becomes 3.
    No other actions have observed effects, so they are treated as no‑ops.

    Parameters
    ----------
    frame : list[list[int]]
        Current board (64 rows × 64 columns) with values 0‑15.
    action : str
        One of 'ACTION1' … 'ACTION7'.
    xy : tuple[int, int] | None
        Click coordinates for ACTION6; unused for other actions.

    Returns
    -------
    list[list[int]]
        Predicted next board (deep copy with applied changes).
    """
    # make a deep copy to avoid mutating the input
    new_frame = [row[:] for row in frame]

    if action == "ACTION5":
        target_row = 53  # zero‑based index as observed
        for col, val in enumerate(new_frame[target_row]):
            if val == 2:
                new_frame[target_row][col] = 3

    # All other actions leave the board unchanged (deterministic no‑op)
    return new_frame

def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Predict the next frame for the observed grid game.

    Observations indicate that ACTION5 changes exactly ONE cell in row 53:
    it turns a value of 2 into 3. The column chosen is the left‑most occurrence
    of a solitary (or start of) run of 2s – i.e. the first column where the
    cell equals 2 and either it is at column 0 or the preceding cell is not 2.

    All other actions leave the board unchanged.
    """
    # deep copy to avoid mutating input
    new_frame = [row[:] for row in frame]

    if action == "ACTION5":
        r = 53  # zero‑based row index observed in all examples
        row_vals = new_frame[r]
        target_col = None
        for c, v in enumerate(row_vals):
            if v == 2 and (c == 0 or row_vals[c - 1] != 2):
                target_col = c
                break
        if target_col is not None:
            new_frame[r][target_col] = 3

    # other actions are no‑ops
    return new_frame

def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Predict the next frame of the grid game.

    Observed rule:
      - ACTION5 changes exactly those cells in row 53 whose current value is 2,
        turning them into 3.
      - In all provided examples there is only one such cell per frame,
        but applying the rule to every 2 in that row is safe and matches the
        observed changes.
      - All other actions leave the board unchanged.

    Parameters
    ----------
    frame : list[list[int]]
        Current 64×64 grid (values 0‑15).
    action : str
        One of 'ACTION1' … 'ACTION7'.
    xy : tuple[int, int] | None
        Click coordinates for ACTION6; unused here.

    Returns
    -------
    list[list[int]]
        New frame after applying the deterministic transition.
    """
    # Deep copy to avoid mutating the input grid
    new_frame = [row[:] for row in frame]

    if action == "ACTION5":
        r = 53  # zero‑based index observed in all ACTION5 examples
        for c, val in enumerate(new_frame[r]):
            if val == 2:
                new_frame[r][c] = 3

    # All other actions are no‑ops
    return new_frame

def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Predict the next frame.

    Observed rule (derived from the examples):
      • ACTION5 flips a single cell that currently holds the value 2 into 3.
      • The flipped cell is always in row 53 when rows are counted starting
        from 1 (i.e. index 52 in zero‑based Python lists).
      • In each frame there is at most one such “2” in that row, so changing
        every occurrence of 2 safely updates exactly the required cell.
      • All other actions leave the board unchanged.

    Parameters
    ----------
    frame : list[list[int]]
        Current 64×64 grid (values 0‑15).
    action : str
        One of 'ACTION1' … 'ACTION7'.
    xy : tuple[int, int] | None
        Click coordinates for ACTION6; unused here.

    Returns
    -------
    list[list[int]]
        New frame after applying the deterministic transition.
    """
    # Deep copy to avoid mutating the input grid
    new_frame = [row[:] for row in frame]

    if action == "ACTION5":
        r = 52                     # zero‑based index for the observed row 53
        for c, val in enumerate(new_frame[r]):
            if val == 2:
                new_frame[r][c] = 3
                break   # only one cell changes per ACTION5

    # Other actions are no‑ops
    return new_frame

def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Predict the next frame.

    Observed rule for ACTION5:
      • In row 53 (zero‑based index) there is exactly one *isolated* cell with
        value 2 – i.e. a 2 whose left and right neighbours are not 2 (or it is
        at a border). That isolated 2 becomes 3.
      • All other actions leave the board unchanged.

    Parameters
    ----------
    frame : list[list[int]]
        Current 64×64 grid (values 0‑15).
    action : str
        One of 'ACTION1' … 'ACTION7'.
    xy : tuple[int, int] | None
        Click coordinates for ACTION6; unused here.

    Returns
    -------
    list[list[int]]
        New frame after applying the deterministic transition.
    """
    # Deep copy to avoid mutating the input grid
    new_frame = [row[:] for row in frame]

    if action == "ACTION5":
        r = 53                     # zero‑based index of the affected row
        row_vals = new_frame[r]
        for c, v in enumerate(row_vals):
            if v == 2:
                left_ok = (c == 0) or (row_vals[c - 1] != 2)
                right_ok = (c == 63) or (row_vals[c + 1] != 2)
                if left_ok and right_ok:      # isolated 2
                    new_frame[r][c] = 3
                    break

    # Other actions are no‑ops
    return new_frame

    """

      • ACTION5 changes a single cell that currently holds the value 2 into 3.
        That cell is always located in row 53 when rows are counted from 1,
        i.e. at zero‑based index 52.
      • In every provided state there is exactly one such “2” in that row,

            if val == 2:

                break   # only one cell changes per ACTION5

    # Other actions are no‑ops
    return new_frame
